title: Research Feature Recommendation Brief: ProductOS V4.1 agentic market intelligence
research_scope: Deep market research across direct PM platforms, indirect work platforms, deep-research products, and CI tools to determine what ProductOS should add to research and auto-research.
market_thesis: The competitive bar has moved from AI drafting to grounded agentic research, recurring market refresh, and explicit controls. ProductOS should differentiate by combining PM-native structure, traceability, validation lanes, and loophole memory.

recommendations.title: Market watchlists with delta-first summaries
recommendations.summary: Let PMs track named competitors, feature shifts, and narrative deltas over time instead of recreating the landscape from scratch.
recommendations.rationale: Crayon, Klue, and Semrush all normalize recurring competitive refresh, while PM teams still need ProductOS-specific implications.
recommendations.implementation_shape: Add competitor watchlists, stored delta history, and implication-first summaries to the research command center.
acceptance_signals: A PM can refresh a competitor set and see only material changes since the last check.
Each delta links back to source note cards with freshness metadata.
guardrails: Do not elevate a delta into a recommendation without checked-date evidence and confidence labeling.

recommendations.title: Guided deep-research plans with scoped sources
recommendations.summary: Introduce an auto-research mode that proposes a plan, lets the PM constrain sources, and then generates a cited report with open questions.
recommendations.rationale: OpenAI, Gemini, and Perplexity are training the market to expect plan-and-execute research rather than static summarization.
recommendations.implementation_shape: Add a plan-preview artifact, source-scope selection, progress state, and report generation that preserves evidence links and confidence.
acceptance_signals: The PM can approve or edit the proposed plan before the run starts.
The final report separates evidence, inference, contradictions, and next actions.
guardrails: Do not start auto-research from unrestricted sources by default.
Do not hide contradictions behind a polished final summary.

recommendations.title: Mixed-source grounding with source-tier awareness
recommendations.summary: Blend public web research, internal ProductOS memory, and structured asks into one evidence graph while keeping source tiers visible.
recommendations.rationale: Klue and Glean both demonstrate the value of mixed-source grounding, while Linear shows that asks and customer attribution matter operationally.
recommendations.implementation_shape: Extend source note cards and retrieval bundles with source tier, freshness, and public versus internal separation before synthesis.
acceptance_signals: ProductOS can explain whether a claim came from public market evidence, internal memory, or customer asks.
The research output can filter or prioritize by source tier.
guardrails: Do not collapse internal and external evidence into one trust label.

recommendations.title: Agent-facing context bridge with retrieval-first mode
recommendations.summary: Expose a retrieval-first context bridge so external or internal agents can pull approved ProductOS context without unrestricted write access.
recommendations.rationale: Notion MCP and adjacent platforms are normalizing agent-facing context access, but ProductOS needs tighter safety boundaries.
recommendations.implementation_shape: Start with retrieval-only context bundles and explicit scope controls before allowing workflow-triggered writes.
acceptance_signals: An agent can consume approved ProductOS context without mutating canonical artifacts.
The PM can see which sources were exposed to the agent.
guardrails: Write access should remain opt-in and separately governed.
Do not expose unapproved workspace content as agent context by default.

recommendations.title: Ralph loop and loophole memory for research outputs
recommendations.summary: Run inspect, review, validate, implement, fix, and revalidate over market-intelligence slices and record rejected paths or loopholes explicitly.
recommendations.rationale: The product market increasingly rewards speed, but ProductOS will lose trust if it cannot explain where auto-research failed or overreached.
recommendations.implementation_shape: Add a Ralph state artifact, validation and manual-review handoff, and a loophole audit that records what ProductOS still misses.
acceptance_signals: Every market-intelligence slice has one explicit proceed or revise outcome.
At least one discovered loophole is recorded when the run exposes a structural weakness.
guardrails: Do not treat schema validity as sufficient for market-intelligence trust.
Do not release auto-research claims without a named manual-review outcome.

recommended_next_action: Ship the V4.1 market-intelligence slice and use the next release to deepen recurring refresh and source controls.
